page: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.85 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 5.33 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a 960 GB local SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is present.

Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 3249.57, placing it in the top 10 percent of tested servers. Multi-core performance is strong, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 77709.84 and a PassMark CPU score of 60061.01, both ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory performance is highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3105.89 and strong cached read-only memory bandwidth of 3479.78 GB/sec. However, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths fall into the average category at 134.73 GB/sec and 68.65 GB/sec, respectively.

This bare metal server offers high resource density with dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for workloads requiring consistent, non-virtualized performance. Strong multi-threaded decompression and PassMark database operations indicate suitability for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications. The qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the combination of 48 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, offering a balanced resource-to-compute ratio for sustained workloads without hypervisor overhead.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and a 2.85 GHz clock speed. It features 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 960 GB of local SSD storage, with no GPU included. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and a top-tier PassMark memory score. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark database operations, and multi-threaded decompression benchmarks all rank in the top 25 percent. Uncached large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput perform at average levels. With its dedicated hardware allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 5.33 GB per core, this server is designed for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring consistent, non-virtualized performance.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring 48 vCPUs via an AMD EPYC 7443P processor, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server with 48 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage, optimized for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining AMD EPYC processing power with high-capacity DDR4 memory and local SSD storage for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 24 physical cores and 48 dedicated vCPUs
256 GB of DDR4 RAM at 3 GHz and 960 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Strong multi-core processing and database operation capabilities
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded processing, and enterprise applications
